political.s: Vermeer Corp reported $150,075 in employee/member contributions to federal candidates in 2024 and has not reported federal lobbying activity. The family-owned company maintains a neutral political posture with minimal federal political engagement.

charity.s: Vermeer Charitable Foundation (established 1958) supports civic, Christian, educational and environmental causes primarily in communities where employees live and work. In 2022, VCF distributed over $1.8 million across local organizations and educational institutions.
charity.focus: Christian/faith-based, education, local community, environmental causes

environment.s: Vermeer manufactures equipment serving environmental management and recycling industries, including grinders and shredders for waste processing. Limited public data on Vermeer's own environmental compliance record or sustainability initiatives.

labor.s: Vermeer employs approximately 3,500-4,000 team members globally with a stated emphasis on team member safety and culture. Employee Net Promoter Score of -18 (from limited sample) suggests potential workplace satisfaction concerns.

dei.s: Vermeer publicly emphasizes diverse team composition and inclusive hiring across global operations. Limited public disclosure of formal DEI policies or metrics.

animals.s: Not applicable. Vermeer manufactures industrial and agricultural machinery, not products tested on animals.

guns.s: Not applicable. Vermeer manufactures construction, mining, and agricultural equipment, not firearms.

privacy.s: Limited public information on Vermeer's data privacy practices. Company website contains standard privacy disclaimers regarding third-party applications and user data handling.

execPay.s: Not disclosed. As a private company, Vermeer does not file SEC proxies or publicly disclose executive compensation details or CEO-to-worker pay ratios.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
